features.title: Product TypeScript scaffold and gate wiring
features.description: Create the runnable Node/TypeScript product skeleton for Posthorn: src entry points, Vitest tests, build output, and npm scripts. This converts the repo from operations-template mode to product mode without weakening the existing engine gates.
acceptance: npm scripts named typecheck, test, build, and lint exist and succeed on a clean checkout.
bash scripts/verify.sh runs in product mode and passes without template-mode skips for lint or unit tests.
src/index.ts exports a minimal Posthorn product entry point and a unit test proves it can be imported.
README development instructions match the scaffold and do not claim unimplemented routes are available.

features.title: Configuration and SQLite storage foundation
features.description: Add Posthorn configuration loading and the default SQLite-backed persistence foundation. The store must support file-backed and in-memory modes so tests stay isolated and deployments remain single-container by default.
acceptance: loadConfig parses documented POSTHORN_* environment variables with defaults and rejects invalid numeric values.
The storage layer opens and closes a SQLite database at POSTHORN_DATA_DIR or :memory: without external services.
A migration or schema initialization path creates the initial tables idempotently.
Unit tests cover defaults, invalid config, in-memory storage, and file-backed persistence using synthetic temp data.

features.title: HTTP server with health and readiness endpoints
features.description: Build the first HTTP surface: a startable gateway process with /healthz and /readyz. Health reports the process is up; readiness proves the storage backend is reachable.
acceptance: createGateway(loadConfig(...)) starts and stops an HTTP server without leaking handles.
GET /healthz returns 200 with a small JSON body and no authentication requirement.
GET /readyz returns 200 when storage can be queried and 503 when storage initialization fails.
Integration tests exercise both endpoints over HTTP against an in-memory store.

features.title: Tenant endpoint management CRUD
features.description: Let tenants register and manage webhook destinations through the documented endpoint routes. This supplies the registered endpoints that message fanout will target.
acceptance: Bearer-authenticated tenants can create endpoints with URL, event type filters, and optional headers.
Tenants can list, fetch, update, and delete only their own endpoints.
Created endpoints receive a whsec_ signing secret that is returned once and persisted hashed or otherwise protected from accidental disclosure.
Tests cover create/list/read/update/delete, tenant isolation, validation failures, and deleted endpoints being hidden from future reads.

features.title: Standard Webhooks signing and verification utilities
features.description: Implement Standard Webhooks compatible signing and receiver verification primitives using HMAC-SHA256 and whsec_ secrets. These utilities are the contract between Posthorn deliveries and customer receivers.
acceptance: Signing produces the expected Standard Webhooks headers for id, timestamp, and signature using whsec_ secrets.
Verification accepts valid raw-body signatures and rejects missing headers, malformed timestamps, stale timestamps, and bad signatures.
Replay-window behavior is configurable with a safe default.
Unit tests include deterministic vectors for valid, invalid, and multi-signature cases.

features.title: Core message intake and fanout queue
features.description: Implement POST /v1/messages so authenticated producers can submit events and Posthorn creates delivery work for matching endpoints. The first version persists messages and pending delivery tasks but leaves retry execution to the worker feature.
acceptance: POST /v1/messages validates eventType and payload, authenticates a tenant API key, and returns 202 for accepted messages.
Accepted messages are persisted with created-at metadata and can be read back through the store.
Fanout creates one pending delivery task for each enabled endpoint whose filter matches the event type.
Tests cover authentication, validation errors, no-match fanout, one-match fanout, and multi-endpoint fanout.

features.title: Crash-safe retry delivery worker
features.description: Add the leased delivery worker that sends pending tasks, signs payloads, and retries failures with exponential backoff. Delivery must be at-least-once and not require Redis or any external broker.
acceptance: The worker claims pending deliveries with a lease so concurrent ticks do not process the same task at the same time.
Successful HTTP delivery marks the task succeeded and records duration and response status.
Failed delivery schedules the next attempt using exponential backoff and dead-letters after the configured attempt budget.
Tests use a local synthetic receiver and cover success, retry, dead-letter, timeout, and lease reclaim behavior.

features.title: Idempotent message intake
features.description: Protect producers from duplicate fanout when they retry a send with the same idempotency key. Reusing a key with the same request returns the original message; reusing it with a different request returns a conflict.
acceptance: POST /v1/messages accepts an optional idempotencyKey and stores the request fingerprint per tenant.
A repeated request with the same key and same body returns the original accepted message without creating new delivery tasks.
A repeated request with the same key and different body returns the documented idempotency_conflict error.
Tests cover tenant scoping, same-body retry, changed-body conflict, and fanout count staying stable.

features.title: Per-attempt audit log
features.description: Expose the delivery-attempt history needed to debug flaky receivers. Each HTTP send attempt records enough structured detail for the tenant API to return a useful audit trail.
acceptance: Every delivery attempt records attempt number, outcome, timestamp, duration, response status when available, and failure reason when available.
GET /v1/messages/:id/attempts returns a paginated newest-first audit log for the authenticated tenant.
Tenants cannot read attempts for another tenant message.
Tests cover success attempts, failed attempts, pagination, and tenant isolation.

features.title: Admin tenant and API-key management
features.description: Implement the disabled-by-default admin control plane for creating tenants and minting or revoking tenant API keys. This is the provisioning path needed before real SaaS teams can use the gateway.
acceptance: Admin routes return 404 unless POSTHORN_ADMIN_TOKEN is configured.
With a valid admin token, operators can create, list, read, update, and delete tenant apps.
Operators can mint, list, and revoke tenant API keys, with new phk_ secrets shown once.
Tests cover disabled admin surface, invalid admin token, app CRUD, key mint/list/revoke, and revoked-key authentication failure.

features.title: Usage metering and quota enforcement
features.description: Track monthly tenant message and delivery-operation usage and enforce configured caps. Tenants need to see their own usage, and admins need a billing read model.
acceptance: Accepted messages increment tenant message usage in the current month.
Each delivery attempt increments tenant delivery-operation usage.
Tenants over a configured monthly message quota receive the documented quota_exceeded error before fanout.
GET /v1/usage and GET /v1/admin/apps/:id/usage return current usage and quota status with tests for month-boundary reset.

features.title: Batch message intake
features.description: Add POST /v1/messages/batch for efficient producer sends of up to 100 events. The route should return per-item results so one invalid item does not hide the outcome of the rest.
acceptance: POST /v1/messages/batch accepts 1 to 100 items and rejects empty or oversized batches.
Each valid item follows the same validation, idempotency, persistence, and fanout behavior as single-message intake.
The response contains per-item ok/error results using the documented stable error codes.
Tests cover mixed success/failure batches, idempotency inside a batch, and no duplicate fanout on retried batches.

features.title: OpenAPI 3.1 contract and closed error codes
features.description: Serve a machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1 contract for implemented routes and pin the public error-code envelope. Client SDKs and external users need the contract to match runtime behavior.
acceptance: GET /openapi.json returns a valid OpenAPI 3.1 document for every implemented public and admin route.
Every non-2xx response uses the documented error envelope with a stable code from a closed schema enum.
Tests compare emitted runtime error codes to the OpenAPI Error.code enum.
The README route table and OpenAPI route list are synchronized for implemented routes.

features.title: TypeScript SDK and command-line client
features.description: Ship the first producer-facing client surface: a zero-surprise TypeScript SDK plus CLI commands for common tenant operations. The SDK and CLI should exercise the same HTTP contract that users see.
acceptance: PosthornClient can create endpoints, send single and batch messages, fetch message status, retry messages, list attempts, and read usage.
The CLI supports create-endpoint, send, list-endpoints, get-message, usage, and help with JSON output for read commands.
Client errors expose status and stable error code without requiring callers to parse raw response bodies.
Tests run the SDK and CLI against the in-process gateway and verify method-to-route coverage.

features.title: Prometheus metrics endpoint
features.description: Expose operator metrics in Prometheus text format so deployments can alert on ingestion, delivery outcomes, dead letters, and process uptime. The endpoint is unauthenticated by design and must not leak tenant payload data.
acceptance: GET /metrics returns Prometheus text exposition with message, delivery, dead-letter, uptime, and build-info metrics.
Metric labels are bounded to documented low-cardinality values and never include tenant IDs, endpoint URLs, or payload fields.
Counters and gauges update when messages are accepted and deliveries succeed, retry, or dead-letter.
Tests pin metric names, labels, and representative values.

features.title: Docker and production compose reference
features.description: Provide the single-container deployment path promised in the product README. Operators should be able to build an image, run it with a mounted data volume, and confirm health locally without adding Redis or Postgres.
acceptance: A Dockerfile builds the Posthorn gateway using npm ci and the compiled distribution.
docker run with POSTHORN_DATA_DIR and POSTHORN_ADMIN_TOKEN starts the service and exposes /healthz.
A docker-compose production reference includes the app service, persistent volume, and Prometheus scrape example without Redis.
Machine checks or documented smoke commands prove the image builds and the container health route responds.

features.title: Event type catalog and consumer portal sessions
features.description: Add the tenant-managed event type catalog and consumer portal session minting route described in the README. These features make endpoint testing and customer-facing portal flows more useful without changing the core delivery path.
acceptance: Tenants can create, list, update, and archive event types with optional schema examples.
Endpoint test sends use a registered event type schemaExample when no explicit payload is supplied and report the payloadSource.
POST /v1/portal/sessions returns a short-lived session token scoped to the tenant and intended endpoint-management surface.
Tests cover event type CRUD, archived event types, test-send example fallback, and portal token expiry.

features.title: Admin and tenant dashboard
features.description: Add a browser dashboard unlocked by POSTHORN_ADMIN_TOKEN and tenant API keys. The dashboard should make tenant provisioning and delivery debugging possible without requiring a CLI.
acceptance: GET /dashboard serves an admin panel for listing tenants, creating tenants, minting keys, and viewing tenant usage.
GET /dashboard/tenant serves a tenant panel for message history, delivery status, and per-attempt audit logs.
Dashboard flows use existing API routes and show loading, empty, error, and success states.
UI evidence includes automated or manual browser screenshots for the main admin and tenant flows.
POST /v1/endpoints/:id/rotate-secret creates a new whsec_ signing secret, returns it exactly once, and marks it active for future deliveries.
Deliveries after rotation include Standard Webhooks signatures for both the new and previous active endpoint secrets during a configurable overlap window.
Expired previous endpoint secrets are not used for new delivery signatures after the overlap window, while the endpoint remains readable without exposing secret material.
Rotation is tenant-scoped, rejects missing or invalid auth, returns 404 for other-tenant endpoints, and does not rotate disabled or missing endpoints incorrectly.
OpenAPI, README route rows, and tests document the implemented route and multi-signature rotation behavior.
features.title: Endpoint signing secret rotation
features.description: Implement zero-downtime endpoint signing secret rotation so tenants can rotate receiver verification secrets without breaking in-flight webhook consumers.

features.title: Endpoint delivery history and stats
features.description: Implement tenant-scoped endpoint delivery history and endpoint delivery stats so customers can debug one receiver without scanning app-wide message history.
acceptance: GET /v1/endpoints/:id/deliveries returns a tenant-scoped, newest-first, keyset-paginated delivery history for one endpoint, including message id, event type, delivery status, attempt count, timestamps, and last error without exposing endpoint secrets or payloads.

GET /v1/endpoints/:id/stats returns endpoint-scoped totals for a trailing window, including delivery counts by status, success rate, average successful delivery duration, per-day trend, and failure reason breakdown.

Both routes require tenant bearer auth, return 404 for other-tenant or missing endpoints, validate pagination/window inputs, and keep disabled/deleted endpoint semantics consistent with existing endpoint reads.
OpenAPI and README route rows mark both endpoint observability routes implemented and document response shapes.
Tests cover tenant isolation, pagination, stats calculations, empty endpoints, invalid query parameters, and no raw secret or payload leakage.

features.title: App-wide delivery listing and filters
features.description: Implement tenant-scoped app-wide delivery listing with useful filters so operators can find failed, pending, or endpoint-specific deliveries without starting from a message id.
acceptance: GET /v1/deliveries returns a tenant-scoped, newest-first, keyset-paginated list of delivery records across the app, including message id, endpoint id, event type, status, attempt count, next attempt time, last error, createdAt, and updatedAt without exposing payloads or endpoint secrets.
The route supports filters for status, endpointId, eventType, and failureReason, plus limit and cursor pagination, with invalid query parameters returning invalid_request.
Filtering by failureReason matches deliveries whose latest error or any recorded attempt failure reason matches the requested bounded reason string.
The route requires tenant bearer auth, returns 405 for unsupported methods, and never exposes other-tenant deliveries or endpoint existence through filters.
OpenAPI, README route rows, and tests document the implemented app-wide delivery listing route and filter behavior.

features.title: Python SDK and webhook verification helper
features.description: Ship the README-promised zero-dependency Python SDK so Python producers can use Posthorn without hand-writing HTTP calls, and Python receivers can verify Standard Webhooks signatures with the same contract as the TypeScript utilities.
acceptance: A Python 3.9+ package exists under clients/python with PosthornClient, PosthornApiError, and verify_webhook using only the Python standard library.

PosthornClient supports tenant API calls for endpoint creation/listing, single and batch message sends, message status, message retry, message attempts, usage, endpoint delivery history/stats, and app-wide delivery listing with pagination/filter arguments.
Client errors expose HTTP status, stable Posthorn error code, and message without requiring callers to parse raw response bodies.

Python webhook verification accepts valid whsec_ Standard Webhooks signatures, supports multi-signature headers, enforces replay windows, and rejects stale, malformed, missing, or mismatched signatures.
Automated tests run the Python SDK against the in-process gateway and cover successful calls, API errors, pagination/filter query arguments, and signature verification failures.
README Python SDK documentation reflects the implemented methods and no longer points at a missing clients/python surface.

features.title: TypeScript SDK parity for implemented tenant routes
features.description: Expand the TypeScript SDK so it covers the implemented tenant routes that are currently HTTP/OpenAPI-only: message listing, endpoint secret rotation, endpoint delivery history/stats, app-wide delivery listing, event type catalog, endpoint test-send, and portal sessions.
acceptance: PosthornClient exposes typed helpers for message listing, endpoint secret rotation, endpoint delivery history, endpoint stats, app-wide delivery listing with filters, event type CRUD, endpoint test-send, and portal session creation.
POSTHORN_CLIENT_ROUTES includes every new helper and the existing route coverage test proves each helper maps to an implemented OpenAPI route.
Client tests exercise representative successful calls for the new helpers against the in-process gateway, including pagination/filter query arguments where applicable.
Client tests cover at least one stable API error path for a new helper without weakening existing API error behavior.
README TypeScript SDK documentation reflects the implemented helper surface and no longer says these tenant helper routes are planned.

features.title: TypeScript admin client
features.description: Implement the README-documented PosthornAdminClient so operators can provision tenants, manage quotas, mint/list/revoke tenant API keys, and read billing usage through the TypeScript package instead of hand-writing admin HTTP calls.
acceptance: The package exports PosthornAdminClient with typed helpers for app create/list/read/update/delete, app usage, API key create/list, and API key revoke.
Admin client helpers authenticate with POSTHORN_ADMIN_TOKEN bearer credentials and reuse PosthornApiError behavior for non-2xx responses.
Admin client route mappings are covered against implemented OpenAPI admin routes without weakening existing tenant SDK route coverage.
Client tests exercise representative admin helper success paths and at least one stable admin error path against the in-process gateway with admin routes enabled.
README Admin / Control-Plane SDK documentation reflects the implemented helper surface and avoids claiming unimplemented admin routes are available.

features.title: Auto-disable persistently failing endpoints
features.description: Implement the README-documented endpoint auto-disable behavior so endpoints that keep dead-lettering deliveries for longer than POSTHORN_ENDPOINT_AUTO_DISABLE_AFTER_MS are automatically taken out of future fanout, while allowing the feature to be disabled with 0.
acceptance: Delivery worker options include endpointAutoDisableAfterMs, defaulting to the documented configuration value, with 0 disabling the behavior.

When a delivery dead-letters, the worker disables that endpoint only if the endpoint has had no successful delivery attempt since the auto-disable cutoff and has at least one failed or dead-letter attempt at or before the cutoff.

Auto-disabled endpoints are excluded from future message fanout and worker claims through the existing enabled=false behavior, while existing endpoint read/list APIs surface enabled=false without leaking payload or secret material.

Tests cover auto-disable after the configured failure window, no disable when the window is disabled, no disable when a recent success resets the failure window, and no disable for a different endpoint or tenant.
README configuration and key-capability text remain truthful for the implemented semantics.

features.title: Message history filters
features.description: Implement the README-planned GET /v1/messages filters so tenants can page message history by event type and created-at windows without exporting all messages client-side.
acceptance: GET /v1/messages supports eventType, after, and before query parameters in addition to limit and cursor, with eventType using the existing event type identifier validation and after/before requiring valid date-time strings.

Message history filters are tenant-scoped, combine with keyset pagination, preserve newest-first ordering, and do not expose idempotency keys, payload hashes, API keys, endpoint secrets, or protected secret metadata.
The TypeScript PosthornClient listMessages helper exposes typed eventType, after, and before inputs and serializes only the allowlisted message-history query keys.
OpenAPI parameters and README route/SDK documentation reflect the implemented message filter surface and no longer describe those filters as planned.
Tests cover eventType filtering, after/before created-at windows, filter pagination, tenant isolation, validation failures, and SDK query allowlisting.

features.title: Admin app system secret rotation
features.description: Implement the planned POST /v1/admin/apps/:id/rotate-system-secret route so operators can rotate an app-level Standard Webhooks signing secret and receive the new whsec_ value exactly once.
acceptance: POST /v1/admin/apps/:id/rotate-system-secret is implemented, disabled when POSTHORN_ADMIN_TOKEN is unset, admin-authenticated before method dispatch, and returns 404 for missing apps.

The route creates a new whsec_ system signing secret, stores it protected at rest, preserves the previous protected secret only for a configurable overlap window, and returns the new secret exactly once without exposing it in app read/list/update responses.
Existing apps with no stored system secret can rotate successfully by creating the first protected system secret without previous-secret overlap metadata.
OpenAPI, TypeScript admin client route mappings/helpers, README API/admin SDK docs, and route coverage reflect the implemented admin route.
Tests cover success, missing app, disabled admin routes, admin auth/method behavior, protected storage/no secret leakage, and legacy app rotation.

features.title: Admin command-line client
features.description: Add a posthorn admin CLI namespace for common control-plane operations so operators can provision tenants, manage API keys, read usage, and rotate app system signing secrets from shell or CI without writing TypeScript.
acceptance: posthorn admin help documents the admin commands and the POSTHORN_URL plus POSTHORN_ADMIN_TOKEN environment variables without requiring credentials.

Admin CLI commands cover common implemented admin routes: create/list/read/update/delete apps, create/list/revoke API keys, read app usage, and rotate app system signing secrets, printing JSON to stdout and one-time secrets only on create/rotate commands.
Missing environment, malformed inputs, and non-2xx API responses return a non-zero exit with actionable stderr that does not leak POSTHORN_ADMIN_TOKEN or one-time secret values.
Tests exercise the admin CLI against the HTTP gateway and pin its route map to implemented OpenAPI admin routes.
README and ROADMAP describe the implemented admin CLI honestly without claiming unimplemented commands.

features.title: Prometheus alerting and Grafana dashboard pack
features.description: Provide the Grafana dashboard and Prometheus alerting-rule artifacts promised by the roadmap so operators can monitor Posthorn metrics without inventing dashboards from scratch.
acceptance: docs include a Prometheus alerting rules file for Posthorn health, dead letters, stuck deliveries, and delivery failure signals using only metrics that the product actually emits.

docs include an importable Grafana dashboard JSON with panels for accepted messages, delivery outcomes, task backlog by status, dead-letter reasons, uptime, and build info using a configurable Prometheus datasource.
Deployment docs and README reference the monitoring artifacts without claiming unsupported production integrations.
Tests validate that the monitoring artifacts are syntactically usable JSON/text files and only reference implemented metric names.
ROADMAP marks the Grafana dashboard / alerting rules portion of the monitoring bullet as shipped without rewriting operator-owned wording beyond shipped markers.
